title: Registry with Static Query
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete class `Registry` so it tracks how many items have been registered and provides a static function to print the count.

## Input
Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10).
Lines 2..N+1: one string per line (item names, no spaces, length ≤ 20).

## Output
Print exactly one line: `Registered: <N>`.

## Requirements
1. `Registry` must have a private `static` member variable `s_count` (initialized to 0).
2. `static void add()` increments `s_count` by 1 (the item name is read by `main` but not stored in the class for this exercise).
3. `static void printCount()` prints `Registered: <s_count>` followed by a newline.
4. `main()` reads N names, calls `Registry::add()` for each, then calls `Registry::printCount()`.
5. Edge case: N = 1 should print `Registered: 1`.

test_cases.label: Hidden: 7 items

title: Score Tracker Bug Fix
prompt_md:
## Goal
The program below is supposed to track the highest score seen across all `ScoreCard` objects using a static member function, but it prints the wrong value. Find and fix the one logic bug.

## Input
Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10).
Lines 2..N+1: one integer score per line.

## Output
Print exactly one line: `Best: <highest_score>`.

## Requirements
1. After reading all scores, `ScoreCard::printBest()` should print the highest score among all values passed to `ScoreCard::submit(int)`.
2. Hidden tests include: all equal scores, a single score, and the maximum appearing last.
3. The bug is a logic error — the program compiles and runs but gives wrong output.
